the sprinter's motion can be modeled using a function that describes their velocity over time as they accelerate and decelerate the velocity function, v(t), is given by:  
v(t) = at³ - bt² + ct + d  
where a, b, c, and d are constants determined through empirical biomechanical analysis the sprinter reaches their maximum velocity at t = 3 seconds, and this maximum velocity is 12 m/s, determine the relationship between the constants a, b, and c the maximum velocity occurs at t = 3 seconds, so the derivative of v(t) at t = 3 is zero:  
v'(t) = 3at² - 2bt + c  
v'(3) = 3a(9) - 2b(3) + c = 27a - 6b + c = 0  
this gives the relationship:  
27a - 6b + c = 0  
the average speed of the sprinter from t = 0 to t = 5 seconds, expressed in terms of the constants a, b, c, and d, is the total distance covered divided by the time taken:  
total distance = ∫₀⁵ v(t) dt = ∫₀⁵ (at³ - bt² + ct + d) dt  
total distance = [ (a/4)t⁴ - (b/3)t³ + (c/2)t² + dt ] from 0 to 5  
total distance = (a/4)(625) - (b/3)(125) + (c/2)(25) + d(5)  
average speed = total distance / 5  
this gives the average speed:  
average speed = (625a/4 - 125b/3 + 25c/2 + 5d) / 5
